title: AAS
description: Analog Agnostic Structure (AAS). A document carries inputs, exactly one of {operationalAmplifier, comparator, instrumentationAmplifier, differenceAmplifier, programmableGainAmplifier, buffer, sampleHold, analogSwitch, multiplexer, adc, dac} (the field name is the discriminator), and outputs. AAS is the home for GENERIC analog/linear ICs that are not power-conversion control parts (those live in CTAS) and not discrete power semiconductors (those live in SAS). Structure mirrors SAS: the device family is discriminated by which top-level field is present, not by a deviceType property. Every valid AAS document is also a valid PEAS document (the `analog` branch).

inputs.description: Design requirements and operating points for this analog IC.
inputs.title: inputs

instrumentationAmplifier.description: Instrumentation amplifier (gain set by R_G / pin / digital).
instrumentationAmplifier.title: instrumentationAmplifier

differenceAmplifier.description: Difference amplifier (fixed on-chip resistor-ratio gain; often extended common-mode range).
differenceAmplifier.title: differenceAmplifier

programmableGainAmplifier.description: Programmable-gain amplifier / PGIA (digitally or pin selectable gain).
programmableGainAmplifier.title: programmableGainAmplifier

buffer.description: Unity-gain / closed-loop buffer, line driver or high-current output buffer.
buffer.title: buffer

integrator.description: Analog integrator / loop compensator (ideal behavioural block).
integrator.title: integrator

summer.description: Analog weighted summer / difference (ideal behavioural block).
summer.title: summer

outputs.description: Computed results per operating point. outputs[i] aligns positionally with inputs.operatingPoints[i].
outputs.title: outputs
